useCases.title: Personalised explanations
useCases.description: Generate alternative explanations of difficult concepts tailored to different learning styles or prior knowledge levels.
useCases.promptExample: Explain photosynthesis to a 12-year-old who already understands that plants make food from sunlight, but doesn't know the chemistry yet.

useCases.title: Quiz and exercise generation
useCases.description: Create practice questions, multiple-choice quizzes, and worked examples from a topic or passage of text.
useCases.promptExample: Create 5 multiple-choice questions about the causes of World War I, each with 4 options and an explanation of the correct answer.

useCases.title: Essay feedback
useCases.description: Provide structured feedback on student writing — structure, argument, evidence — but factual accuracy must be verified.
useCases.promptExample: Give structured feedback on this GCSE history essay paragraph. Focus on: argument clarity, use of evidence, and historical accuracy. [paste paragraph]

useCases.title: Curriculum planning
useCases.description: Draft lesson outlines, scheme of work structures, and differentiation ideas — always reviewed by a professional educator.
useCases.promptExample: Create a 6-lesson scheme of work for introducing fractions to Year 5 pupils. Include learning objectives, key activities, and assessment ideas.

useCases.title: Story and script drafting
useCases.description: Generate first drafts, explore plot alternatives, write dialogue, or develop character backstories — human creativity drives the final work.
useCases.promptExample: Write the opening scene of a short story set in a lighthouse during a storm. Tone: tense, literary fiction. First person, present tense.

useCases.title: Lyric and poetry writing
useCases.description: Compose song lyrics, poems, or spoken word pieces — excellent for generating ideas and drafts to rework.
useCases.promptExample: Write a verse for a blues song about a failed road trip. Use the AAB rhyme scheme and include a car metaphor.

useCases.title: Style exploration
useCases.description: Rewrite a passage in a different author's style, literary era, or genre for creative exploration.
useCases.promptExample: Rewrite this paragraph in the style of Raymond Carver: sparse language, short sentences, understated emotion. [paste paragraph]

useCases.title: Email and document drafting
useCases.description: Draft professional emails, letters, meeting summaries, and reports quickly — always reviewed and edited before sending.
useCases.promptExample: Draft a polite but firm follow-up email to a contractor who has not responded to our invoice query in two weeks.

useCases.title: Meeting notes and summaries
useCases.description: Paste a transcript or rough notes — AI structures them into action points, decisions, and follow-ups.
useCases.promptExample: Summarise this meeting transcript into: decisions made, action items (with owners), and outstanding questions. [paste transcript]

useCases.title: Policy and procedure drafts
useCases.description: Generate first drafts of internal policies — must be reviewed by qualified professionals for legal and organisational accuracy.
useCases.promptExample: Draft a social media policy for a small charity with 20 staff. Include: acceptable use, personal accounts, and incident reporting.

useCases.title: Patient information leaflets
useCases.description: Generate plain-language explanations of conditions or procedures — must be clinically reviewed before use.
useCases.promptExample: Write a plain-language explanation of type 2 diabetes for a patient with low health literacy. Avoid jargon. Max 200 words.

useCases.title: Care plan structuring
useCases.description: Help structure person-centred care plan templates and prompts — specific clinical decisions must be made by qualified professionals.
useCases.promptExample: Create a template for a person-centred support plan for an adult with learning disabilities. Include sections for: strengths, goals, daily routine, and risk management.

useCases.title: Research summarisation
useCases.description: Summarise academic papers or guidelines — always check against the original source as summaries can omit nuance or hallucinate details.
useCases.promptExample: Summarise the key recommendations from this NICE guideline extract in plain language for a general audience. [paste text]

useCases.title: Code generation and completion
useCases.description: Write functions, debug errors, explain code, and suggest refactors across most programming languages.
useCases.promptExample: Write a Python function that takes a list of dictionaries with 'name' and 'score' keys, and returns the top 3 by score, sorted descending.

useCases.title: Code explanation and documentation
useCases.description: Explain what complex code does in plain language, generate docstrings, or produce README documentation.
useCases.promptExample: Explain what this function does line by line, then write a docstring for it. [paste code]

useCases.title: Architecture and design advice
useCases.description: Discuss system design trade-offs, suggest patterns, and help evaluate approaches — final decisions require human engineering judgment.
useCases.promptExample: I'm building a notification system for 100,000 daily users. Compare an event-driven queue approach versus polling. What are the trade-offs?

useCases.title: Marketing copy and campaigns
useCases.description: Draft ad copy, social media posts, email campaigns, and product descriptions — great starting points that need human polish.
useCases.promptExample: Write 3 variants of a Facebook ad for a local gym's January membership offer. Each variant should have a different angle: value, community, transformation.

useCases.title: Market research synthesis
useCases.description: Summarise and synthesise market research documents — hallucination risk is real, always verify statistics and claims against original sources.
useCases.promptExample: Summarise the key trends and opportunities from these three market research excerpts. Highlight contradictions between sources. [paste text]

useCases.title: Business plan sections
useCases.description: Draft executive summaries, market analysis sections, and value propositions — requires thorough fact-checking of any statistics cited.
useCases.promptExample: Write a 300-word executive summary for a B2B SaaS product that automates staff scheduling for care homes. Include: problem, solution, market opportunity, ask.

useCases.title: Lyrics and song structure
useCases.description: Generate verses, choruses, bridges, and full song structures — especially useful for writer's block or exploring different styles.
useCases.promptExample: Write a chorus and two verses for an indie folk song about leaving a hometown. Key themes: nostalgia, ambivalence, growth. AABB rhyme scheme.

useCases.title: Production notes and briefs
useCases.description: Create reference descriptions for session musicians, mix engineers, or collaborators — describing the sonic vision and mood.
useCases.promptExample: Write a production brief for a track that sounds like late-night drive music. Reference artists for tone, describe the instrumentation and mood.

useCases.title: Music theory explanations
useCases.description: Explain chord progressions, modes, rhythm patterns, and music theory concepts — at any level of complexity.
useCases.promptExample: Explain why the I-IV-V-I chord progression sounds 'resolved'. Use plain language, then show the same concept using music theory terminology.

useCases.title: Essay planning and structuring
useCases.description: Help develop argument structures, identify counterarguments, and outline essay plans — the writing and thinking should be your own.
useCases.promptExample: Help me plan an essay arguing that social media has had a net negative effect on teenage mental health. Suggest 3 main arguments and potential counterarguments for each.

useCases.title: Concept explanations
useCases.description: Get alternative explanations when lecture notes or textbooks aren't clear — always cross-reference with course materials.
useCases.promptExample: I don't understand confidence intervals. Explain it as if I understand basic statistics (mean, standard deviation) but not hypothesis testing.

useCases.title: Revision tools
useCases.description: Generate flashcard questions, practice exam questions, and self-quiz materials from your notes.
useCases.promptExample: Create 10 flashcard-style questions from these revision notes. Each question should test understanding, not just recall. [paste notes]

useCases.title: Research starting points
useCases.description: Get an overview of a topic and identify angles to explore — never cite AI as a source, and always verify any facts independently.
useCases.promptExample: Give me an overview of the debates around universal basic income, including the main arguments for and against from different political perspectives.
